title: hieracium vulgatum subsp. aspernatum
intro: Hieracium vulgatum subsp. aspernatum, also known as aspernate hawkweed, is a subspecies of plant belonging to the dandelion family that is native to certain regions. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits associated with Hieracium vulgatum subsp. aspernatum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and act as a diuretic. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be guided by a qualified healthcare professional due to potential side effects.
